response:
Short answer
We switched because reusing the existing Rackspace Identity flow was simpler and more reliable for calling the Fleet/APIM paths we need — it aligned with the APIM/Rackspace auth model and avoided the configuration and operational friction we were hitting with the Entra client‑credential approach.

Why (details)
- Existing flow already worked with APIM: APIM expects/validates Rackspace tokens and maps caller types/roles, then forwards trusted headers (X-User-Type, X-DDI, etc.) to backends. Using the Rackspace Identity token is therefore the natural/compatible auth for the targeted Fleet/APIM endpoints.
- Operational reliability: the Entra client‑credential approach required extra Azure auth configuration (AUTH_TENANT_ID / AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES and proper audience values) and had caused auth failures in prod when missing or mis‑set. Reusing the Rackspace identity helper avoided that class of environment/auth config fragility.
- Reuse and simplicity: we already have shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(...) and RACKSPACE_USERNAME/PASSWORD in place, so switching avoided adding and rotating new Entra client secrets and new scope management.
- Deployment behavior: some remediation flows rely on APIM keeping/forwarding Rackspace identity context; a backend-to-APIM call using a Rackspace token fit better with how the platform authenticates operator calls than a backend-held Entra app token.

Known tradeoff
- Rackspace identity token responses do not include tenant DDI/x-tenant-id for operator/Racker accounts, so tenant context must be supplied from platform data (customer_subscriptions.ddi or equivalent) rather than inferred from the token. We accepted that tradeoff given the reliability/compatibility benefits.
context_injected: {"ns": "azure-msp", "task": "debug", "facts": ["Require approval gates for production changes to AI systems; no direct commits to main branch without PR review → implemented_by → PR review", "Single pane of glass → integrates_with → Cyber Recovery", "Single pane of glass → deployed_to → Azure", "Single pane of glass → contains → Immutable backup vaults", "Single pane of glass → implements → PCI-DSS", "Single pane of glass → managed_by → Rackspace", "Contoso Financial → uses → Project Sentinel", "Contoso Financial → expressed_interest_in → Adatum Insurance", "Contoso Financial → uses → Azure Monitor DCR", "Contoso Financial → declined → Northwind Traders", "Contoso Financial → targets → SOC2", "Contoso Financial → in_legal_review_with → Woodgrove Bank"], "recent": ["2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Fleet Event Grid remediation token acquisition was revised again on 2026-04-09: instead of Entra client credentials, backend-py now reuses the existing Rackspace Identity flow backed by RACKSPACE_USERNAME and RACKSPACE_PASSWORD. shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py calls shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') and sends that token as Authorization: Bearer to APIM for the targeted Event Grid ensure endpoint.\n[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3; component: backend-py switched auth from Entra client credentials to Rackspace Identity flow; env_var", "2026-04-08: On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upser", "2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.\n[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediat", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFOR", "2026-04-02: On 2026-04-02 in /Users/benj8956/Documents/RackspaceClaw, added the first actual Entra-aware proxy implementation assets for the RackspaceClaw POC. Added runtime-config/proxy/docker-compose.proxy.yml, oauth2-proxy.cfg.template, default.conf.template, proxy.env.example, TLS cert placeholder docs, and docs/entra-proxy-poc.md. The proxy stack uses nginx in front of oauth2-proxy with provider=entra-id and passes X-Auth-Request-Email to the upstream, matching OpenClaw trusted-proxy guidance. Also updated scripts/serve_poc.py so the upstream honors trusted-proxy identity headers and exposes proxy co", "2026-03-23: Rackspace Identity token mint responses do not return x-tenant-id/DDI for operator/Racker accounts. The identity response is only a token mint/lookup payload; x-tenant-id must be supplied from platform context such as customer_subscriptions.ddi or another authoritative customer mapping, not inferred from the minted token response. For Fleet Platform operator API calls that use x-auth-token, do not expect the mint response to provide tenant context.", "2026-03-19: In azr-compliance-v3, X-Auth-Token authorization is defined in APIM, not OpenAPI. APIM validates the Rackspace token, classifies the caller as racker or customer, maps racker named-value groups to MSP-Admin/MSP-Engineer/MSP-Viewer, maps customer roles to admin/billing/observer, enforces route/category RBAC in api-policy.xml, then forwards trusted headers like X-User-Type, X-Auth-Method, X-Access-Level, X-User-Roles, and X-DDI to the backend. The actual racker group display-name values are environment-configured APIM named values, not stored in repo.", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, targeted Event Grid remediation API failures in prod had two independent causes. First, azr-fleet-platform-prod was missing AUTH_TENANT_ID and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES, causing backend function auth to return 'JWT validation is not configured; AUTH_TENANT_ID/AZURE_TENANT_ID missing' and 503 Authentication service unavailable for http_eventgrid_subscription_targeted. Fix: set AUTH_TENANT_ID=570057f4-73ef-41c8-bcbb-08db2fc15c2b and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES=api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f,3eabce94-dd29-4358-b262-09a43f2ddb60 on azr-fleet-platform-prod a", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.178 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/register. Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly score block. Updated global WAF custom rules by moving BlockKnownPath from priority 5 to 6 and adding A", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, Event Grid eligibility was tightened so Event Grid subscriptions are only created/maintained for monitoring-entitled service blocks. Changes were made locally in source_data, eventgrid activity/orchestrators, onboarding, validation, http_eventgrid, and OpenAPI docs. Customer-wide Event Grid orchestration now uses only Lighthouse subscriptions with computed_service_tier='advanced'; ensure_event_subscription_single now skips non-lighthouse or non-monitoring subscriptions; the targeted Event Grid API returns 409 for subscriptions without monitoring entitlement"]}
